Stub implementations for action menu functions in the Pebble firmware test suite.

# Purpose
The code defines a set of functions for managing an action menu system, which is part of a user interface library. The functions provide operations to create, open, and manage action menus and their hierarchical levels. The code includes functions to open an action menu with a given configuration, retrieve the root level of an action menu, and manage the freezing and unfreezing of the menu. It also provides functions to set the alignment of the menu, close the menu, and retrieve labels and action data from menu items.

The code is intended to be used as part of a larger application, as indicated by the inclusion of headers from the `applib/ui` directory. It does not implement the functionality directly but provides a set of function prototypes that return default values, such as `NULL` or `false`. This suggests that the code serves as a placeholder or interface definition for the action menu system, which will be implemented elsewhere. The functions defined in this code are likely to be used by other parts of the application to interact with the action menu system, making it a crucial component for user interface management.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/ui/action_menu_window.h`
- `applib/ui/action_menu_hierarchy.h`
